figures.caption: FIG. 2. Fermi volume as a function of J . (a) h-wave magnets in 2D. (b) j-wave magnets in 3D. The vertical axis is the Fermi volume in units of 8 √ 2π(mµ)3/2/(3ℏ3). The horizontal axis is J in units of ε0. Cyan curves represent numerical results based on the tightbinding model. Red lines represent the analytic reult V0 based on the continuum model. Purple curves represent analytic results based on the continuum model up to the second order in J.We have set ℏ2/ ( ma2 ) = ε0/4 and µ = 0.1ε0.
